name: Paskha
recipeInstructions:
1. Line the paskha mold or clean 7 5x5 inches plastic or unglazed flower pot with drainage hole with three double-thicknesses of cheesecloth. Place on a wire rack set over a cake pan.
2. Combine 1/2 pound cottage cheese, sugar, and eggs in a food processor or blender. Process until smooth, scraping the sides as necessary. Pour into a medium-sized saucepan and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly for 6 minutes or until the mixture reaches nearly boiling point or an instant-read thermometer registers 150°F. Stir in vanilla extract. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. In two batches, add remaining cottage cheese, farmer's cheese, jam, butter, lemon rind, and food coloring (if using) to a clean food processor or blender. Process until smooth, whisking into the bowl with the cooked cheese mixture. Stir in raisins, orange peel, and almonds.
4. Pour the mixture into the prepared mold. Fold any excess cheesecloth over the top and cover with plastic wrap. Place a flat-bottomed plate or saucepan slightly smaller than the diameter of the mold on top to weight it down. Refrigerate for 3 hours, then weigh the top with two 23-ounce cans or a heavy iron skillet. Allow to drain in the refrigerator until the center is firm, up to one week.
5. Unmold and remove cheesecloth. Garnish the mold with candied fruits and sliced almonds. Slice into wedges and serve as cheesecake or spread on Kulich.
